goal: v6.2.0 (prove-smooth-learn): measurement + friction removal + outcome feedback — review-yield query, a record mutation CLI, smart Stop-gate re-execution, derived eval counts, and minimal outcome feedback — no new subsystems

acceptance_criteria.criterion: record CLI: set-status / add-evidence / add-ac / outcome mutate the record with validation + atomic write; malformed input is a clean error, not a crash
record mutations preserve schema and round-trip (a mutated record still passes check-record)
smart Stop-gate: a terminal stop whose canonical diff+status are unchanged since the last green verify skips re-execution; any change re-runs the full umbrella
derived eval counts: the count lint computes the real total from the suites and checks docs against it (no hand-set constant to drift)
control-report --review-yield computes per-release findings→fixes + outcome tally from records
outcome feedback: record outcome writes the field + appends .quality-loop/outcomes.jsonl; brief surfaces the tally

constraints: No new subsystems; net-simpler where possible (record CLI replaces hand-JSON; derived counts remove a drift class)
Stage 3 (outcome) stays one field + one log + one brief line — defer if it needs more
Backward compatible: existing CLI flags unchanged; new subcommands are additive
Every claim states only what the code enforces
non_goals: Running the six live §6.2 benchmark cells (needs claude -p — operator action; deliver the runnable path + review-yield instead)
New hosts / hosted service / MCP / dashboards / mutation engine / extra review rounds / config surface beyond the 3 keys
implementer: claude-fable-5-orchestrator (fable core lane + opus hook/control/docs lane)
verification_plan: All suites green after each lane + integration
Fresh-context cross-family review (Fable xhigh + Codex) on the diff

validation_contract.goal: v6.2.0 lands measurement + friction removal + outcome feedback with net-simpler surface, each behavior eval-pinned and each claim honest about what the code enforces
evidence: 247 core + 36 add-on + 33 npm suites green; derived per-suite count lint green
Two cross-family fresh-context reviews (Codex + Fable) -> 6 findings fixed -> confirmation approve on functional + security axes
verify umbrella green at done with both reviews attested to a matching diff hash

independent_review.notes: Two fresh-context reviews (Codex cross-family + Fable xhigh) on the change set both returned request_changes; they jointly caught 6 findings incl. the headline blocking bug (smart Stop-gate skip bypassing a would-FAIL umbrella via a post-verify record mutation, since the diff hash excludes .quality-loop/). All fixed: marker now binds record_sha256; mutations refuse to persist a newly-invalid record; count lint checks per-suite arithmetic; docs sharpened. A focused fresh-context confirmation returned approve on functional + security axes with all suites green (247 core + 36 add-on + 33 npm) and every fix eval-pinned.

security_review.notes: Security surface: the new record mutation CLI writes only status/commands_run/acceptance_criteria/outcome (never diff_sha256/attested_at/verdict/fresh_context) and now refuses to persist a newly-invalid record; the smart Stop-gate marker is bound to diff + record + status and documented honestly as tamper-evident (agent-writable, CI is the unconditional anchor), not a forgery boundary; outcome notes are secret-redacted. security_verdict: approve.

completion_record.goal: Ship v6.2.0 (prove-smooth-learn): measurement (review-yield + runnable §6.2), friction removal (record CLI, smart Stop-gate, derived counts), and minimal outcome feedback — no new subsystems
done_when: 247 core gate cases (20 static + 61 behavioral + 32 memory + 50 reality + 30 routing + 54 hook) + 36 add-on + 33 npm all pass; derived count lint (now per-suite) green
Two fresh-context cross-family reviews caught 6 findings incl. 1 headline blocking bug; all fixed and eval-pinned; confirmation review approve on functional + security
completion_record.right_size_decision: minimal_new_code — net-simpler: record CLI replaces hand-JSON (and shrinks tamper surface), derived counts delete a hand-set constant + drift class, canonical_diff_sha256 collapses 3 hash copies into 1. New code confined to sharpening existing gates.
evidence: All suites green (see done_when); 10/10 recorded evidence rows re-execute clean
record CLI dogfooded to record this task's own evidence; review-yield query produces real per-release numbers
smart Stop-gate marker binds diff+record+status; regression evals pin the record-mutation and would-pass-skip paths
risks: Marker is agent-writable (tamper-evident, not forgery-proof); CI never reads it and re-executes unconditionally — the anchor
The six §6.2 benchmark runs remain an operator action (spec committed); execution not part of this release
completion_record.rollback: git revert the squash-merge of the v6.2.0 PR (single revert point). No data migration, no external state.
follow_ups: Run the six §6.2 micro-task bench cells (bench/tasks/14-micro-bugfix.json) — the named next milestone
Re-render docs/images evidence PNG to v6.2.0 counts

callers_checked: stop_gate -> reads marker -> skips umbrella on match
count lint -> canonical_gate_cases() computed from suites
patterns_to_follow: atomic write + validate for record mutation

minimality_decision.reason: Net-simpler where it counts: the record CLI REPLACES hand-written JSON surgery (and shrinks the tamper surface — structured writes vs arbitrary Bash), derived counts DELETE a hand-maintained constant + a doc-sweep drift class (proven during this very integration: zero manual count edits), and canonical_diff_sha256 collapses three copies of diff-hashing into one. New code is confined to: 4 record subcommands, a read-only review-yield query, a latency-only stop-gate short-circuit, and one optional outcome field. No new subsystem.

rejected_lower_rungs.note: memory-recall coupling for outcomes (kept to one field + one ledger + one brief line)
a bench-run orchestration engine (the six §6.2 cells stay a one-command operator action)

plan: Lane 1 core (fable): record CLI + outcome, last-verified marker written by verify, derived-count lint, own evals
Lane 2 (opus): smart stop-gate reading the marker, review-yield query, hook/control evals, docs + CHANGELOG + version bumps
Integrate: reconcile counts, rebuild npm dist, full suite
Cross-family review -> fix -> attest -> done -> ship

review_findings.finding: Smart Stop-gate skip could bypass a would-FAIL umbrella (marker keyed on diff+status; record excluded from diff hash)
review_findings.resolution: fixed: marker binds record_sha256; _verified_clean requires it; regression eval case_stop_gate_reruns_umbrella_on_record_mutation; skip test uses a would-pass stub

review_findings.finding: Record mutations not validated — set-status done returned 0 on a check-record-invalid record
review_findings.resolution: fixed: record_validation_issues extracted; verbs refuse to write a newly-invalid record; eval case_record_mutation_refuses_invalid_result

review_findings.finding: Count lint checked only the total — a wrong per-suite addend passed
review_findings.resolution: fixed: lint checks each breakdown addend + the six-suite sum; eval proves a wrong addend under a right total is caught; stale constant-name prose removed

review_findings.finding: Dogfood record schema-invalid (minimality rationale vs reason; map omitted CONTRIBUTING.md)
review_findings.resolution: fixed: reason + rejected_lower_rungs; map covers changed docs; check-record passes

review_findings.finding: stop-gate docs overclaimed 'never allow a stop the umbrella would block'; skip note omitted 'record'
review_findings.resolution: fixed: docs state tamper-evident/CI-anchored; skip note now 'diff + record + status unchanged'

next_action: Release: commit, push, PR, merge, tag.

outcome.note: Post-ship defect in the release's own headline feature: control-report --review-yield double-counted the latest release (live/archive twin) on its first real invocation, and the archived v6.1.0 record it read was itself schema-invalid. Both caught by the v6.3.0 outside-audit review and fixed there (PR #18). No revert needed; counted as a post-ship escape per docs/review-yield-2026-07-20.md.
